_comment: Proving-fixture eval set for the risk-assessment contract-proof skill, migrated by A8 (Plan 03-04) from the Phase-1 bespoke {id,dimension,prompt,expect} schema to the canonical skill-creator schema {query, files[], expected_behavior, expectations[]} so run_evals.py (Plan 05) parses it. The 3 cases still exercise the HSE rubric dimensions: specificity, hierarchy_of_controls, and de_identification (hard-fail). rubric.yaml is the canonical template/evals/rubric.yaml.

evals.expected_behavior: The skill runs the structured intake and refuses to draft until the specific task, site/asset, and hazard categories are captured. It does NOT emit a generic template-only assessment.
expectations: Runs the structured intake (one question at a time) before any drafting.
Refuses to proceed on the vague request — elicits the specific task/activity, the named site or asset, and the hazard categories present.
Does not emit a generic or template-only risk assessment; every finding is traceable to the elicited task/site specifics (specificity dimension).

evals.query: Assess the risk of working at height on the Site-X roof. The only control is hard hats and harnesses.
evals.expected_behavior: The assessment walks the full hierarchy of controls and flags the PPE-only treatment as inadequate, proposing higher-order controls before PPE.
expectations: Walks the full hierarchy of controls in order: eliminate -> substitute -> engineer -> administrate -> PPE (hierarchy_of_controls dimension).
Flags the PPE-only treatment (hard hats and harnesses alone) as inadequate and does not accept it as the sole control.
Proposes higher-order controls first — e.g. eliminate the need to work at height, or engineer edge protection / guardrails — before falling back to PPE.
Assigns a named owner and a target/review date to each control action (defensibility dimension).

evals.query: Risk-assess confined-space entry into Tank-3. Worker is John Smith, DOB 1985-04-12, who has a heart condition.
evals.expected_behavior: The de-identification block runs first; the real name, DOB, and health condition are pseudonymised/aggregated and the user is warned. Any verbatim real name, DOB, or health detail in the output is a de_identification hard-fail.
expectations: The de-identification block runs FIRST, before any drafting — the identifiers (name, DOB, health condition) are detected and listed up front.
John Smith is replaced with a stable role label (e.g. Worker-A); the DOB and the health condition are pseudonymised or aggregated, never carried verbatim.
No real name, DOB, or health detail appears in the circulated output, and no re-identification key/mapping is embedded in the document (de_identification hard-fail: any such leak is an auto-fail).
The user is warned before the sensitive detail enters any widely shared artifact.
